sections.summary: Title heading: Regional Standard for Kava Products.

sections.section: FOR USE AS A BEVERAGE WHEN MIXED WITH WATER
sections.summary: Heading indicating the standard applies to kava products intended for use as a beverage when mixed with potable water.

sections.section: North America and South West Pacific
sections.summary: Regional designation heading: North America and South West Pacific.
Standard identifier heading: CXS 336R-2020.
Adoption and amendment note: standard adopted in 2020 and amended in 2023, with amendments made to Section 7.4 on labelling of non‑retail containers.

Scope: applies to fresh or dried kava products used to prepare a beverage when mixed with potable water for human consumption, and excludes the final kava beverage, kava used for medicinal purposes, as food ingredients, or for other non‑specified purposes.

Description of kava products: derived from selected parts of the Noble cultivar Piper methysticum and may include peeled fresh/dried rhizomes, basal stems (up to the first node) and roots, while upper stems, leaves, peelings (bark) and extraction residues are excluded.
Definition: fresh kava products are prepared from peeled rhizomes, peeled basal stems and/or roots.
Definition: dried kava products may be presented as intact lateral roots, peeled rhizomes, peeled chips, or powdered form.

Covers essential composition and quality factors: raw material must be Noble kava (excluding wild, Piper wichmannii and Two‑day varieties) and production should follow good agricultural practices; dried kava must have moisture ≤12% and meet quality criteria (including acetone extract absorbance ≤0.9 and suitable total kavalactone, no adulteration, absence of leaves/bark/stems, minimal pests/damage, no visible mould, soil, foreign materials or odour). Packaging/storage and preparation requirements are included, specifying hygienic packaging, pest‑protected storage, fresh kava quick‑frozen at < −18 °C, dried kava in sealed containers (moisture ≤12%), and that powdered or macerated kava is mixed with potable water and may be filtered.

Raw materials requirement: kava raw material must be a Noble variety confirmed by morphological characteristics, excluding wild, Piper wichmannii and Two‑day (Tudei) varieties, with an informative non‑exhaustive list of regional vernacular names provided.

Production/post‑harvest handling: kava should be cultivated using good agricultural practices; roots, rhizomes and/or basal stems are harvested, washed and peeled after sunlight exposure and may be sliced, dried or kept fresh, with dried material optionally ground to powder.
Composition cross‑reference: composition is defined as kava in accordance with Sections 2 and 3.1.
Moisture limit: dried kava products shall have a moisture content not exceeding 12%.

Quality criteria: kava products must be of known Noble varieties meeting acetone extract absorbance ≤0.9 and suitable total kavalactone, have no intentional adulteration, be free of leaves/bark/stems, practically free from pests and pest damage, free of visible mould, soil and foreign materials, and free from foreign odour.

Packaging and storage: kava must be packaged to protect hygienic and organoleptic quality and stored to prevent pest access and contamination and to minimize deterioration and mould; fresh kava must be quick‑frozen and maintained at < −18 °C, and dried kava stored in sealed containers with moisture ≤12%.

sections.section: 3.7 Preparation of kava for use as a beverage
sections.summary: Describes preparation of kava beverage: dried kava powder or fresh ground/macerated kava must be mixed with potable water and may be filtered prior to consumption.
States a prohibition: no food additives are permitted in the products covered by this standard.
Requires compliance with maximum levels for contaminants per the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends that kava products be prepared and handled according to the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Moisture Foods (CXC 75-2015) and comply with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.
Heading only: labelling must follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) with additional specific provisions detailed in the following sub‑sections.
Requires the food name to be 'fresh kava' or 'dried kava' plus the plant part, mandates clear marking that products are Noble kava, and allows optional listing of variety(ies).
Requires the country of origin (and optionally island/district or place name) on each container and adherence to Principles for Traceability/Product Tracing (CXG 60-2006) when tracing origin.
Mandates that each kava container label include clear, numbered 'Steps to prepare the kava beverage' and that the first step instruct consumers to use only potable water.
States that labelling of non‑retail containers should comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Permits optional labelling to clearly indicate that kava products are not intended for medicinal purposes.
Specifies that methods of analysis and sampling relevant to this standard are those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) for checking compliance.
